title: Cboe Global Markets, Inc. (CBOE) — Intrinsic Value & Prospect Score
description: An independent discounted cash flow (DCF) intrinsic value analysis of Cboe Global Markets (CBOE) compiled entirely by Gemini 3.1. Economic Prospect Score: 75/100 — Strong Prospect.

fcfGrowth.title: FCF Growth Rate (Y1-Y5)
fcfGrowth.rationale: A 6.5% growth rate is projected, driven by the structural growth in options trading volumes, particularly short-dated options, offset by potential periods of low volatility. The expansion of data and access solutions provides a steady, recurring revenue base.

discountRate.title: Discount Rate (WACC)
discountRate.rationale: An 8.5% discount rate reflects Cboe's stable cash flows and deep economic moat, balanced against the inherent cyclicality of trading volumes tied to macroeconomic market volatility.

terminalGrowth.title: Terminal Growth Rate
terminalGrowth.rationale: A 2.5% terminal growth rate aligns with long-term macroeconomic GDP growth expectations, recognizing that exchange operators are fundamentally tied to the broad growth of financial markets.

thesisParagraphs: Cboe Global Markets operates one of the most enviable business models in finance: a toll bridge for risk management and speculation. Through its exclusive licenses for S&P 500 index options (SPX) and its ownership of the VIX methodology, Cboe has established a near-monopoly in the trading of broad market volatility. This structural advantage ensures that whenever macroeconomic uncertainty rises, Cboe's transaction revenues follow suit.

While the company faces intense competition in standard equity routing and multi-listed options, its core proprietary products provide a highly durable, high-margin revenue stream. The recent structural shift towards zero-days-to-expiration (0DTE) options has further entrenched Cboe's utility for both institutional hedgers and retail speculators. However, the current valuation implies an expectation of persistently high volatility and volume, leaving little margin of safety if markets enter a prolonged, subdued period.
verdictFooter: Based on FY2025 actuals. Data via S&P Global (Mar 20, 2026).

faq.question: Why did Gemini pick a 6.5% growth rate for Cboe?
faq.answer: Gemini projects 6.5% FCF growth based on the continued secular trend of increased options usage, particularly 0DTEs, alongside steady growth in their recurring data and access solutions businesses.

faq.question: What are the main risks to this valuation?
faq.answer: The primary risk is a prolonged 'volatility crush'—a sustained period of calm, rising equity markets which typically depresses trading volumes in Cboe's highly lucrative VIX and SPX products.

faq.question: Is Cboe's moat really that strong?
faq.answer: Yes. Its moat is rooted in exclusive intellectual property rights to the most heavily traded index products globally. Replicating the liquidity pool and regulatory approval for a competing product is nearly impossible.

prospectVerdictDetail: Cboe Global Markets holds a unique and powerful position in the financial exchange industry, primarily driven by its proprietary products like the VIX and SPX options. Its moat is fortified by significant regulatory and intellectual property barriers, alongside deep liquidity networks. While broader market volatility heavily influences its short-term results, its long-term trajectory remains solid as it expands into digital assets and international markets. The consistent free cash flow generation and strategic acquisitions bolster its moderate-to-strong prospect standing.

competitiveMomentum.title: Competitive Momentum
competitiveMomentum.summary: Cboe demonstrates steady competitive momentum, largely reliant on the sustained popularity of its exclusive index products and its ability to capture trading volumes in shifting market conditions.

factors.name: Revenue Growth vs. Peers
factors.rationale: Cboe's revenue growth is generally consistent but can be lumpy, heavily dependent on market volatility which drives trading volumes. It performs well against peers but isn't a hyper-growth tech entity.

factors.name: Market Share Trajectory
factors.rationale: The company maintains a dominant share in index options trading due to its exclusive licenses for SPX and VIX. However, competition in the broader equities and standard options space from rivals like Nasdaq and ICE is fierce.

factors.name: Pricing Power
factors.rationale: Cboe exercises significant pricing power over its proprietary, exclusive index products, allowing for margin protection. However, pricing power is more constrained in the highly commoditized multi-listed options and equities markets.

factors.name: Product Velocity
factors.rationale: While Cboe innovates with new product variations (like zero-days-to-expiration or 0DTE options which have seen massive adoption), the core exchange infrastructure business moves deliberately rather than with rapid technological velocity.

moatDurability.title: Moat Durability
moatDurability.summary: Cboe's economic moat is exceptionally durable, built upon exclusive intellectual property, deep liquidity pools, and significant regulatory hurdles that deter new entrants.

factors.name: Switching Costs
factors.rationale: For institutional traders and market makers, the technological integration and routing systems required to trade on Cboe's platforms create moderate-to-high switching costs, though less absolute than enterprise software.

factors.name: Network Effects
factors.rationale: Financial exchanges benefit from massive network effects: liquidity begets liquidity. Cboe's deep pools of buyers and sellers for options contracts make it the default venue, effectively preventing fragmentation of its core markets.

factors.name: Regulatory & IP Position
factors.rationale: The company's moat is arguably defined by its IP. Its exclusive licenses to create options on the S&P 500 Index (SPX) and its ownership of the VIX methodology grant it a legally protected monopoly in these highly lucrative segments.

factors.name: Capital Intensity Advantage
factors.rationale: Operating electronic exchanges requires significant, ongoing investment in technology infrastructure and cybersecurity to ensure low latency and high reliability, making it moderately capital intensive.

sentimentCatalyst.title: Sentiment & Catalysts
sentimentCatalyst.summary: Market sentiment toward Cboe is generally favorable, supported by steady cash generation, though it can fluctuate with macroeconomic expectations regarding market volatility.

factors.name: Earnings Estimate Revisions
factors.rationale: Earnings estimates tend to be stable with upward revisions typically following periods of heightened market volatility which spike trading volumes and, consequently, clearing and transaction fees.

factors.name: News & Narrative Sentiment
factors.rationale: The narrative remains constructive, focusing on the continued structural shift toward options trading among both retail and institutional investors, heavily featuring Cboe's 0DTE products.

factors.name: Management & Capital Allocation
factors.rationale: Management has demonstrated prudent capital allocation, balancing strategic acquisitions (like BATS Global Markets and digital asset platforms) with consistent dividends and share repurchases.

prospectKeyRisks: A sustained period of exceptionally low market volatility could depress trading volumes and transaction revenues.
Intense competition from other exchange operators and off-exchange trading venues could pressure fees in multi-listed products.
Regulatory changes impacting the structure of options markets or the licensing of index products.
prospectKeyCatalysts: Continued explosive growth in retail and institutional adoption of short-dated (0DTE) index options.
Successful integration and scaling of its international acquisitions and digital asset ventures.
Periods of macroeconomic uncertainty or market stress that drive elevated hedging activity and VIX trading volumes.
prospectMethodology: Consensus Analysis — Economic Prospect Score averaging independent evaluations from Opus 4.6 and Gemini 3.1. Gemini scored CBOE at 75/100 and Opus at 74/100. Each factor score is the arithmetic mean of both models. Three pillars: Competitive Momentum (0-35), Moat Durability (0-35), and Sentiment & Catalysts (0-30).
